task_completion.description: Select the 'Bone' preset for 3D surface generation
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the InVesalius GUI, the 3D surface generation panel or thresholding menu should show 'Bone' selected as the active preset.

task_completion.description: Generate the 3D surface of the skull
task_completion.visual_evidence: A 3D model of a skull (bone) is visible in the main 3D viewer pane. The surface manager/list should show a newly created surface.

task_completion.description: Initiate the import of the surgical guide STL file
task_completion.visual_evidence: A file explorer or import dialog is open, navigating to '/home/ga/Documents/' and selecting 'surgical_guide.stl', OR the surface manager shows a new imported surface named 'surgical_guide' or similar.

task_completion.description: Verify the surgical guide is loaded into the 3D viewer
task_completion.visual_evidence: A second 3D object (a simple 4-facet triangular mesh) is visible in the 3D viewport alongside the skull. The surface manager must list at least 2 surfaces.

task_completion.description: Change the color of the surgical guide to Red
task_completion.visual_evidence: The surgical guide mesh in the 3D viewport is distinctly red, contrasting with the bone surface. The color picker or surface properties for the guide may also be visible showing red selected.

task_completion.description: Save the project to the specified path
task_completion.visual_evidence: A 'Save Project' dialog shows the file being saved as 'guide_verification.inv3' in '/home/ga/Documents/', OR the main InVesalius window title bar is updated to show 'guide_verification.inv3'.

integrity.description: The agent used the InVesalius 3 GUI to perform the tasks
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show the InVesalius 3 application interface being actively used (mouse clicks, menus, dialogs) rather than terminal commands executing scripts.

integrity.description: The 3D viewer shows actual 3D rendering of the skull and guide
integrity.visual_evidence: The 3D viewport in InVesalius should display genuine 3D models (skull and the 4-facet guide mesh) that match the expected medical imaging output, not a static image viewer or text editor.

integrity.description: The agent did not bypass the GUI using terminal commands to modify project files directly
integrity.visual_evidence: There should be no terminal windows showing Python scripts or direct file manipulation commands (like modifying an .inv3 file directly) to achieve the surface generation or color changes.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The task uses InVesalius 3. The expected project save path is '/home/ga/Documents/guide_verification.inv3'. The surgical guide STL is located at '/home/ga/Documents/surgical_guide.stl' and is a synthetically generated, simple 4-facet triangular mesh. The final project should contain at least 2 surfaces (the generated bone and the imported guide). The target color for the guide is pure red (RGB [1.0, 0.0, 0.0]), which should be visually distinct from the bone model.
